summary: Martin Fowler announces that JetBrains has launched their Early Access Program for a C# development tool. He humorously notes that they didn't follow his naming suggestion and called it ReSharper instead. Initial feedback from his colleagues at Thoughtworks has been positive, though they're eager for additional features.
key_points: JetBrains launched an Early Access Program for their C# refactoring tool
The tool was named ReSharper, ignoring Fowler's naming input
Early adopters among Fowler's colleagues are enthusiastic but want more features
money_quotes: Sadly they ignored my naming advice and instead called it ReSharper
Early noise from my colleagues has been enthusiastic if still wanting more
key_insight: JetBrains' entry into C# tooling with ReSharper signals growing demand for refactoring tools beyond the Java ecosystem.
